title: Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam
tagline: Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam — spiritual town of Andhra Pradesh

heroImage.alt: Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam, Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Rama located in the town of Buchireddypalem in SPSR Nellore District, in Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Rama located in the town of Buchireddypalem in SPSR Nellore District, in Andhra Pradesh, India. The temple covers an area of 2 acres. The temple has two entries, of which one is prominent and has a larger gateway tower called as Gopuram. Another entry has a small door on the right side of the temple.

topPlaces.name: Buchireddypalem
topPlaces.description: Buchireddypalem is a town in the Nellore district of Andhra Pradesh . It is a municipality located 15 km from the Nellore city, and it is a part of the Kovur Assembly constituency.

topPlaces.name: Vavveru
topPlaces.description: Vavveru is a village in Buchireddypalem Mandal, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh, India. It is located on the eastern shore of Kanigiri Reservoir, about 13 kilometres northwest of the district seat Nellore and 5 kilometres north of the mandal seat Buchireddypalem.

topPlaces.name: Rebala
topPlaces.description: Rebala is a village located in the Buchireddypalem mandal of Nellore district, Andhra Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Jonnawada
topPlaces.description: Jonnawada is a village in Buchireddypalem mandal, located in Nellore district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is located on the banks of river Pennar.

topPlaces.name: Damaramadugu
topPlaces.description: Damaramadugu is an agricultural village located in Buchireddipalem mandal of Nellore district, Andhra Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Yellayapalem
topPlaces.description: Yellayapalem is located in the Kodavalur sub-district in the Nellore district in the state of Andhra Pradesh. The Indian census reports a population of 8215, with 2060 households.

items.note: Buchireddypalem is a town in the Nellore district of Andhra Pradesh . It is a municipality located 15 km from the Nellore city, and it is a part of the Kovur Assembly constituency.

Vavveru is a village in Buchireddypalem Mandal, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh, India. It is located on the eastern shore of Kanigiri Reservoir, about 13 kilometres northwest of the district seat Nellore and 5 kilometres north of the mandal seat Buchireddypalem.
Rebala is a village located in the Buchireddypalem mandal of Nellore district, Andhra Pradesh, India.
Jonnawada is a village in Buchireddypalem mandal, located in Nellore district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is located on the banks of river Pennar.
Damaramadugu is an agricultural village located in Buchireddipalem mandal of Nellore district, Andhra Pradesh, India.
Yellayapalem is located in the Kodavalur sub-district in the Nellore district in the state of Andhra Pradesh. The Indian census reports a population of 8215, with 2060 households.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tirupati Airport (108 km) and the nearest railway station is Tirupati Railway Station (113 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sri Kodandarama Swami Devastanam?
faq.a: The top attractions are Buchireddypalem, Vavveru, Rebala, Jonnawada, Damaramadugu.
